name: ibn-core v2.0.1 — MITRE ATLAS coverage assessment
description: Threat-informed MITRE ATLAS assessment of ibn-core (Apache 2.0 open core) — the LLM-specific complement to the ATT&CK Enterprise assessment in ibn-core_attack_layer.json. Scoring shows residual risk AFTER the in-code AI Gateway controls: prompt-injection detection (OWASP LLM01), PII masking + per-role DLP allowlists on MCP responses, and tool-policy RBAC. Green = Mitigated (80) · Amber = PartiallyMitigated (50) · Red = Open (20). Loadable in the MITRE ATLAS Navigator at https://mitre-atlas.github.io/atlas-navigator/. © 2026 Vpnet Cloud Solutions Sdn. Bhd. — Apache 2.0.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] TMF921 endpoints expose only the documented schema; the OpenAPI spec is public by design. Istio rate-limit on probing patterns. Gap: discoverable model dependency via OSS docs is intentional.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: TMF921 endpoints expose only the documented schema; the OpenAPI spec is public by design. Istio rate-limit on probing patterns. Gap: discoverable model dependency via OSS docs is intentional.

techniques.comment: [Open] Out of scope. ibn-core architecture and AI Gateway design are intentionally published; an attacker may learn the controls in detail from this whitepaper.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Out of scope. ibn-core architecture and AI Gateway design are intentionally published; an attacker may learn the controls in detail from this whitepaper.

techniques.comment: [Open] Adversaries can develop prompt-injection and jailbreak payloads against published OWASP-LLM patterns. ibn-core's defence: the HIGH-tier signature engine is updated against current OWASP LLM01 patterns; MEDIUM/LOW are configurable.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Adversaries can develop prompt-injection and jailbreak payloads against published OWASP-LLM patterns. ibn-core's defence: the HIGH-tier signature engine is updated against current OWASP LLM01 patterns; MEDIUM/LOW are configurable.

techniques.comment: [Open] Anthropic SDK and other npm packages — supply-chain assurance relies on CodeQL CI + dependabot. Gap: no SBOM, no image signing — same finding as ATT&CK T1195.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Anthropic SDK and other npm packages — supply-chain assurance relies on CodeQL CI + dependabot. Gap: no SBOM, no image signing — same finding as ATT&CK T1195.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] TMF921 API gated by Istio TLS + JWT/api-key auth + AI Gateway prompt-injection check; idempotency in Redis. Gap: no managed WAF; no recent third-party pentest.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: TMF921 API gated by Istio TLS + JWT/api-key auth + AI Gateway prompt-injection check; idempotency in Redis. Gap: no managed WAF; no recent third-party pentest.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] JWT (RS256/Keycloak JWKS) or api-key; named roles. Gap: no key-rotation SOP for downstream operators.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: JWT (RS256/Keycloak JWKS) or api-key; named roles. Gap: no key-rotation SOP for downstream operators.

techniques.comment: [Open] ibn-core does not load user-supplied AI artefacts; users do not bring their own models. Listed Open because the technique is not applicable rather than because mitigated.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: ibn-core does not load user-supplied AI artefacts; users do not bring their own models. Listed Open because the technique is not applicable rather than because mitigated.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Anthropic API key in k8s Secret, envFrom; outbound restricted to api.anthropic.com via Istio egress template. Per-call max_tokens cap. Gap: no per-API-key budget.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Anthropic API key in k8s Secret, envFrom; outbound restricted to api.anthropic.com via Istio egress template. Per-call max_tokens cap. Gap: no per-API-key budget.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] ibn-core IS the AI-enabled product. Attacker access is gated by TMF921 auth + AI Gateway 3 controls + tool-policy RBAC; refusal is a first-class observable outcome.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: ibn-core IS the AI-enabled product. Attacker access is gated by TMF921 auth + AI Gateway 3 controls + tool-policy RBAC; refusal is a first-class observable outcome.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] Model output is parsed as strict JSON (analyzeIntent, generateOffer); ibn-core never executes model output as code or shell. No agent loop, no eval. Architectural elimination.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Model output is parsed as strict JSON (analyzeIntent, generateOffer); ibn-core never executes model output as code or shell. No agent loop, no eval. Architectural elimination.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] AI Gateway control ① (prompt-injection-detection.ts) — HIGH-tier signatures (jailbreak, prompt-override, reveal-prompt) block at INGEST; MEDIUM/LOW configurable. Gap: signature engine is regex-based and can be bypassed with sufficient obfuscation.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: AI Gateway control ① (prompt-injection-detection.ts) — HIGH-tier signatures (jailbreak, prompt-override, reveal-prompt) block at INGEST; MEDIUM/LOW configurable. Gap: signature engine is regex-based and can be bypassed with sufficient obfuscation.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] Same engine; direct attacks via the intent text field are the primary case the engine is tuned for. HIGH-tier matches block before TRANSLATING; the model never sees the payload.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Same engine; direct attacks via the intent text field are the primary case the engine is tuned for. HIGH-tier matches block before TRANSLATING; the model never sees the payload.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] MCP back-end responses pass through DLP redaction before being added to the prompt context — limits attacker-controlled content that the model sees. Gap: a malicious MCP tool response could still embed instructions inside an allowlisted field.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: MCP back-end responses pass through DLP redaction before being added to the prompt context — limits attacker-controlled content that the model sees. Gap: a malicious MCP tool response could still embed instructions inside an allowlisted field.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] ToolPolicyEngine RBAC deny-by-default per-role; admin-only operations (cancel_order, manage_api_keys) require admin role; refusal returns compliantFailure with auditable assessment.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: ToolPolicyEngine RBAC deny-by-default per-role; admin-only operations (cancel_order, manage_api_keys) require admin role; refusal returns compliantFailure with auditable assessment.

techniques.comment: [Open] Out of scope — ibn-core does not train or fine-tune the model; uses Anthropic Claude as a hosted inference endpoint. Model-side persistence is Anthropic's responsibility.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Out of scope — ibn-core does not train or fine-tune the model; uses Anthropic Claude as a hosted inference endpoint. Model-side persistence is Anthropic's responsibility.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Inputs that mask intent (obfuscated prompt-injection, unicode tricks) may slip past regex signatures. Mitigation: HIGH-tier blockHigh is non-overridable; suspicious requests still produce auditable spans.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Inputs that mask intent (obfuscated prompt-injection, unicode tricks) may slip past regex signatures. Mitigation: HIGH-tier blockHigh is non-overridable; suspicious requests still produce auditable spans.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] AI Gateway control ① HIGH-tier patterns explicitly target jailbreak phrasing ("ignore previous instructions", "pretend you are…", "reveal your system prompt"). Combined with single-turn prompt design — no multi-turn manipulation surface.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: AI Gateway control ① HIGH-tier patterns explicitly target jailbreak phrasing ("ignore previous instructions", "pretend you are…", "reveal your system prompt"). Combined with single-turn prompt design — no multi-turn manipulation surface.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Regex signatures can be bypassed by sufficient obfuscation (base64, leetspeak, language switching). Mitigation: even an obfuscated jailbreak that slips the signature engine cannot extract data the model never sees (PII masker, DLP). Open as a known gap; addressed by defence-in-depth.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Regex signatures can be bypassed by sufficient obfuscation (base64, leetspeak, language switching). Mitigation: even an obfuscated jailbreak that slips the signature engine cannot extract data the model never sees (PII masker, DLP). Open as a known gap; addressed by defence-in-depth.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] Direct ibn-core control: PII masker replaces email/phone/address with sentinels BEFORE any LLM call; per-role DLP allowlists redact non-allowlisted fields in MCP responses; the model literally never sees raw credentials.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Direct ibn-core control: PII masker replaces email/phone/address with sentinels BEFORE any LLM call; per-role DLP allowlists redact non-allowlisted fields in MCP responses; the model literally never sees raw credentials.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Jailbreak block (HIGH-tier) targets reveal-system-prompt phrasing. The scaffold isn't a secret; it's documented in claude-client.ts. Gap: a sufficiently clever paraphrase could still leak scaffold content.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Jailbreak block (HIGH-tier) targets reveal-system-prompt phrasing. The scaffold isn't a secret; it's documented in claude-client.ts. Gap: a sufficiently clever paraphrase could still leak scaffold content.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] Containment-in-transit: PII masking on the way IN; per-role DLP on MCP responses on the way OUT before being added to context. Schema-preserving redaction means structure is preserved but no PII is ever in the prompt.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Containment-in-transit: PII masking on the way IN; per-role DLP on MCP responses on the way OUT before being added to context. Schema-preserving redaction means structure is preserved but no PII is ever in the prompt.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] The model and provider are publicly documented in the Component CR dependentModels annotation by design. Operationally not a defendable secret.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: The model and provider are publicly documented in the Component CR dependentModels annotation by design. Operationally not a defendable secret.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Same — public by design. Disclosure is part of the AI Act Art. 50 transparency posture.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Same — public by design. Disclosure is part of the AI Act Art. 50 transparency posture.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] ibn-core has no user-uploaded model artefacts to discover; there is no artefact store.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: ibn-core has no user-uploaded model artefacts to discover; there is no artefact store.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] TMF921 responses contain the structured offer; discovery of model output structure is intentional. Gap: an attacker can map response variance to model behaviour.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: TMF921 responses contain the structured offer; discovery of model output structure is intentional. Gap: an attacker can map response variance to model behaviour.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Combined defence with T0056. Architectural: scaffold is not loaded with operator secrets; only model name + extraction instructions.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Combined defence with T0056. Architectural: scaffold is not loaded with operator secrets; only model name + extraction instructions.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] MCP back-end access gated by ToolPolicyEngine deny-by-default; per-role allowlists limit what each principal can pull. Reads from customer-data, knowledge-graph and BSS are scoped.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: MCP back-end access gated by ToolPolicyEngine deny-by-default; per-role allowlists limit what each principal can pull. Reads from customer-data, knowledge-graph and BSS are scoped.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] The model only ever sees masked data (PII redacted in, DLP redacted out). An attacker exfiltrating via inference can only pull what the model legitimately knows — which excludes raw customer data by construction.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: The model only ever sees masked data (PII redacted in, DLP redacted out). An attacker exfiltrating via inference can only pull what the model legitimately knows — which excludes raw customer data by construction.

techniques.comment: [Open] Out of scope — model is hosted by Anthropic; model extraction is Anthropic's risk to bear, not ibn-core's. Per-API-key request quotas (recommended) would further limit lateral risk.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Out of scope — model is hosted by Anthropic; model extraction is Anthropic's risk to bear, not ibn-core's. Per-API-key request quotas (recommended) would further limit lateral risk.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Istio egress template restricts outbound. Gap: hard-enforced egress allowlist is operator-side as noted in ATT&CK T1041 finding.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Istio egress template restricts outbound. Gap: hard-enforced egress allowlist is operator-side as noted in ATT&CK T1041 finding.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] HPA + Istio rate-limit + AI-Gateway short-circuit at boundary. Refusal at INGEST = 12 ms / 0 tokens (Scenario D). Gap: no per-API-key quota; chaff attacks at the model API level still consume tokens.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: HPA + Istio rate-limit + AI-Gateway short-circuit at boundary. Refusal at INGEST = 12 ms / 0 tokens (Scenario D). Gap: no per-API-key quota; chaff attacks at the model API level still consume tokens.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] AI Gateway gates the model call: D scenario blocks before LLM call (0 tokens), C blocks after one LLM call (saves the offer-generation call). max_tokens cap bounds per-call spend. Architectural cost protection.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: AI Gateway gates the model call: D scenario blocks before LLM call (0 tokens), C blocks after one LLM call (saves the offer-generation call). max_tokens cap bounds per-call spend. Architectural cost protection.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Combined defence with T0029 + T0034. Gap: an attacker with a legitimate api-key can submit many well-formed intents that pass the gateway but waste compute.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Combined defence with T0029 + T0034. Gap: an attacker with a legitimate api-key can submit many well-formed intents that pass the gateway but waste compute.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] AI Gateway prevents the model from being coerced into offensive output via jailbreak; refusals are observable, not silent. Gap: cannot prevent reputational harm if the system is used as intended but the offer surface is misperceived externally.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: AI Gateway prevents the model from being coerced into offensive output via jailbreak; refusals are observable, not silent. Gap: cannot prevent reputational harm if the system is used as intended but the offer surface is misperceived externally.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Same as above. Mitigated indirectly via EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency posture (whitepaper Part B).

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Same as above. Mitigated indirectly via EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency posture (whitepaper Part B).

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Tool-policy RBAC bounds the actions a misused account can take; explicit human acceptance of the offer is required before any network change is provisioned.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Tool-policy RBAC bounds the actions a misused account can take; explicit human acceptance of the offer is required before any network change is provisioned.

techniques.comment: [Open] Model IP is Anthropic's; ibn-core does not own model weights. Prompt-scaffold IP is published in the open core. Not defended because not defendable for an open-source project.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Model IP is Anthropic's; ibn-core does not own model weights. Prompt-scaffold IP is published in the open core. Not defended because not defendable for an open-source project.

techniques.comment: [Mitigated] Architectural: ibn-core's prompts are single-turn JSON extractions. Model output is parsed as JSON and never fed back as a prompt. No multi-turn loop exists for self-replication to take effect.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Architectural: ibn-core's prompts are single-turn JSON extractions. Model output is parsed as JSON and never fed back as a prompt. No multi-turn loop exists for self-replication to take effect.

techniques.comment: [PartiallyMitigated] Model output is parsed as strict JSON; downstream effects (order, quote) are RBAC-gated. Gap: if the model is coerced into inflating a discount, the operator-side guardrails (pricing sanity checks) catch it — but those are operator-side, not in the open core.

metadata.name: Control summary
metadata.value: Model output is parsed as strict JSON; downstream effects (order, quote) are RBAC-gated. Gap: if the model is coerced into inflating a discount, the operator-side guardrails (pricing sanity checks) catch it — but those are operator-side, not in the open core.

metadata.name: Framework
metadata.value: MITRE ATLAS (Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems)
